title: wedelia hoffmanniana
intro: Wedelia hoffmanniana, also known as Mexican dandelion or wild plantain, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family that is native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal effects. Its leaves and flowers are rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like potassium and iron, offering various health benefits such as reducing fever, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. Wedelia hoffmanniana can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is often used in Ayurvedic and traditional Mexican medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
